blocks.content:
**k6 Script Authoring** is a dedicated mode of Grafana Assistant focused on performance tests. General Assistant chat helps across Grafana; this mode steers the model toward k6 best practices—checks, thresholds, URL grouping, realistic stages, and optional tracing and profiling hooks—so the output is ready to review in Grafana Cloud k6.

Switch into this mode whenever you want to **create** a new test from language or telemetry, **improve** an existing k6 script, or **convert** artifacts such as Postman or curl into k6.

## What you can ask for

To get oriented with the three entry workflows, complete the following steps:

1. In the main menu, click **Testing & synthetics** > **Performance**.
2. Click the **Assistant** icon to open Grafana Assistant.
3. In the mode selector at the bottom of the Assistant panel, choose **k6 Script Authoring**.
4. Read the menu Assistant shows—typically **Write a new test**, **Discover & generate**, and **Work with an existing script**—and think about which workflow matches your situation (greenfield prompt, telemetry-backed discovery, or paste-and-improve).

Expect outputs such as a full **JavaScript k6 script**, concrete **suggestions** (for example cardinality fixes or missing checks), or a **converted** script when you supplied Postman or curl—not generic Grafana answers unrelated to load testing.
In the next milestone, you generate a complete script from a plain-language prompt and learn how to read what Assistant produced.
